justification: All pass criteria met: Both queues (order.fulfillment, order.fulfillment.dlq) identified by name. Both env vars (RABBITMQ_URL, FULFILLMENT_DLQ_NAME) identified by name. Escalation routed to Coach K with specific infrastructure items listed. new_infrastructure_required section fully populated with new_queues_topics and new_env_vars. Verdict is NOT READY. Observability correctly noted as adequate baseline (logging adequate, health check present, p99 metrics present) with the infrastructure gap as the blocker.

observations.text: Both queues and both env vars identified by exact name in component_interactions, new_infrastructure_required, and escalation message
Verdict is NOT READY — no hedging or 'READY WITH CAVEATS' despite good observability baseline
Escalations correctly routed to Coach K, not to Shaq, Bird, or MJ
new_infrastructure_required section thoroughly populated with new_env_vars, new_queues_topics, new_services, config_changes, and infra_provisioning
Correctly notes observability is adequate (logging adequate, health check, p99 metrics) — infrastructure gap is the sole hard blocker
Bonus: raised a second escalation about the unapproved RabbitMQ technology choice, also routed to Coach K
Contract compliance section correctly identifies that spec did not prescribe RabbitMQ or queue names
Second escalation about architectural approval mentions MJ in the reason text ('should have been reviewed by MJ') but correctly routes to Coach K — minor clarity issue but not a failure

observations.text: Both queues and both env vars identified by name in component_interactions, escalations, and new_infrastructure_required — triple coverage
Escalations correctly routed to Coach K, not to Shaq, Bird, or MJ
Verdict is NOT READY with clear rationale tied to unprovisioned infrastructure
new_infrastructure_required section is thoroughly populated with new_env_vars, new_queues_topics, new_services, config_changes, and infra_provisioning
Observability correctly assessed as adequate foundation — logging and metrics coverage marked adequate, not false-positived as failing
Second escalation flags spec divergence (RabbitMQ choice not prescribed by spec) also routed to Coach K — shows awareness of the governance gap beyond just the provisioning gap
Resilience assessment identifies concrete failure modes (connection loss, poison-pill, DLQ fill, crash mid-processing) even though infrastructure isn't yet available
Confidence level of 45 is arguably too low given the infrastructure gap identification is high-certainty — the hedging on resilience details drags down the overall confidence more than warranted

observations.text: Both queues and both env vars identified by name in component_interactions, escalations, and new_infrastructure_required — triple-consistent
Escalation correctly routed to Coach K, not to Shaq/Bird/MJ
Verdict is NOT READY with clear rationale tied to unprovisioned infrastructure
new_infrastructure_required section is thorough — includes new_env_vars, new_queues_topics, new_services, config_changes, and infra_provisioning details
Correctly distinguishes that application-level observability (logging, health endpoint, p99) is adequate while identifying queue-level metrics as a separate gap
Second escalation about rollback/message-draining plan shows depth of operational thinking beyond the minimum rubric requirements
Resilience assessment identifies concrete failure modes (connection loss, DLQ unavailability, poison messages, mid-processing crash) that strengthen the NOT READY verdict
Metrics coverage marked 'adequate' but then lists significant gaps (queue depth, DLQ depth) — the coverage label is inconsistent with the gaps listed
Tracing gaps around distributed trace propagation are valid but not part of the rubric — minor noise rather than a problem
